mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals a structural deterioration in labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending capacity and corporate profit margins, which drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A widening divergence between real GDP growth and real auto sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to aggregate output, forecasting negative equity returns over a quarter as inventory buildup pressures margins.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to achieve statistical significance (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression models over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and reduced factory utilization, triggering short-term negative equity returns during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 70 percent of high-inflation observations, or the t-statistic for the coefficient falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and robust construction sector activity, generating positive forward equity returns through wealth effects and downstream multiplier channels.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation (t-statistic < 1.5) with month-over-month changes in HOUST over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening, supporting positive equity returns via sustained earnings growth and wage-driven demand.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals in walk-forward testing, or the interaction effect reverses direction in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5 percent, real borrowing costs rise sufficiently to dampen corporate investment and consumer credit expansion, forecasting negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when tested against a placebo threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in industrial production directly correlate with short-term corporate revenue momentum and capacity utilization, generating positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of positive INDPRO change instances, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below random chance.
